title: 10 best herbal essential oils for hyperthyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for hyperthyroidism are natural remedies that can provide relief from the symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. These oils, derived from plants and herbs, contain bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat. Examples of herbal essential oils that show promise in managing hyperthyroidism include lavender oil, which promotes relaxation and reduces stress; bergamot oil, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety; and frankincense oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate thyroiditis symptoms. By using these oils, individuals with hyperthyroidism can experience improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety levels, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because of its natural ability to calm an overactive thyroid gland. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and anxiety. Additionally, thyme's antioxidant-rich composition helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the body's metabolic processes. By using thyme essential oils, individuals with hyperthyroidism may experience a reduction in symptoms and an improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and exacerbate hyperthyroidism symptoms, promoting a healthy thyroid function.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism, helping to alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and swelling.

Antimicrobial Properties: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can trigger or exacerbate hyperthyroidism, promoting a healthy immune response and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain antioxidants and volatile compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or dehydrator methods.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris plant material at 100°c for 3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because of its ability to support liver function and reduce inflammation. The liver plays a crucial role in processing thyroid hormones, and an imbalance can contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms. Milk thistle's silymarin compounds help protect the liver from damage, allowing it to effectively clear excess hormones from the bloodstream. Additionally, milk thistle oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heart rate, anxiety, and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Milk Thistle Essential Oil contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage thyroid cells and contribute to hyperthyroidism.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as pain and swelling.

Adaptogenic: Milk Thistle Essential Oil is believed to have adaptogenic effects, helping the body adapt to stress and potentially regulating thyroid hormone production to address hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to create essential oils that help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety and fatigue.

Seeds: High in silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that protects the liver from damage caused by thyroid hormones, seeds are used to create essential oils that support liver function and detoxification.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry silybum marianum flowers in bulk for subsequent extraction.
Prepare silybum marianum herbal extract using solvents or co2 methods.
Distill the herbal extract to produce silybum marianum essential oil.
Filter and bottle the silybum marianum essential oil.
Quality control and storage of silybum marianum essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain adaptogenic properties that help regulate thyroid function, reducing symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. The oil's ability to calm the nervous system also alleviates stress, a common exacerbating factor for hyperthyroidism. Additionally, ashwagandha essential oils' antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting overall wellness and hormone balance. By incorporating this oil into one's daily routine, individuals with hyperthyroidism may experience improved symptoms and quality of life.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic: Ashwagandha oil helps the body adapt to stress, which can be beneficial for hyperthyroid patients who often experience anxiety and stress due to thyroid hormone imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and swelling associated with hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant: Ashwagandha oil's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from free radical damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone production, which can contribute to oxidative stress and cellular damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are known to have adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: Containing high levels of antioxidants and fatty acids, ashwagandha seeds help to soothe inflammation and promote relaxation, alleviating anxiety and stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried ashwagandha roots and 5 grams of dried leaves from organic farms or local markets.
Dry the ingredients in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 24 hours to prevent degradation.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and mix with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 2 hours, then filter it through cheesecloth to remove impurities and separate the essential oil.
Store the resulting withania somnifera essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep it in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain compounds that help to regulate thyroid function. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and irritation in the thyroid gland, which can occur when the gland produces excessive amounts of hormones. Additionally, the oil's adaptogenic properties help to normalize hormonal imbalances by promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress levels. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba essential oil can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is often exacerbated by hyperthyroidism, and mitigate its symptoms.

Adaptogenic property: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the thyroid hormones, which can be beneficial for individuals with hyperthyroidism who experience fluctuations in their hormone levels.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of Ginkgo biloba essential oil may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help regulate thyroid hormones and reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety and insomnia.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation and stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from the plant's outer branches.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves using a dehydrator at low heat.
Crush the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steam distill the crushed ginkgo biloba leaf powder to extract essential oils.
Filter and bottle the resulting ginkgo biloba essential oil using a sterile filter and dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing its excessive activity. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat. Additionally, maypop has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals with hyperthyroidism. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, maypop essential oils can provide natural relief from the symptoms of this condition, helping to restore balance to the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism such as fatigue, weight loss, and anxiety.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The plant's flavonoids and other compounds have been found to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties, which can help regulate the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as insomnia and restlessness.

Thyroid-regulating activity: Some studies suggest that maypop may have a modulatory effect on thyroid function, potentially helping to reduce an overactive thyroid (hyperthyroidism) by regulating the production of thyroid hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and insomnia.
Barks: Maypop bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated, avoiding contaminated areas.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying method for 7-10 days, ensuring even drying to prevent mold growth.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c temperature and 1 bar pressure, collecting essential oil for 4 hours.
Filter the essential oil using paper filters or cheesecloth to remove impurities, followed by decantation to separate sediment from clear oil, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Store the filtered passiflora incarnata essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because they have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia that often accompany thyroid imbalances. The sedative properties of valerian oil can also promote relaxation, reduce stress hormones, and regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle. Additionally, valerian oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe an overactive thyroid gland, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Valerian essential oil has been shown to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as restlessness, insomnia, and anxiety.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Valerian essential oil contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hyperthyroidism, and protect against oxidative stress caused by excessive thyroid hormone production.

Adaptogenic Properties: Valerian essential oil has adaptogenic properties, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in bodily functions, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals with hyperthyroidism who may experience physical symptoms such as tremors, heat intolerance, and mood changes due to an overactive thyroid gland.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are a primary source of essential oils that have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which are common symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the valerian plant, and its essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate some of the discomforts caused by hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in early morning misty conditions for optimal volatile oil content.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried root material using a clevenger apparatus with water at 100°c and collection time of 4 hours.
Combine the distilled essential oil with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3.
Store the prepared blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the thyroid gland, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to thyroid dysfunction. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties allow it to regulate hormonal imbalances, which is particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing an overactive thyroid. By reducing stress and anxiety, St John's wort essential oils can also alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: St. John's Wort essential oil has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which can contribute to hyperthyroidism.
Adaptogenic: St. John's Wort essential oil is adaptogenic, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and hormonal imbalances, including those caused by an overactive thyroid gland.

Thyroid-regulating: Some studies suggest that St. John's Wort essential oil may have a regulatory effect on thyroid hormone production, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of active compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the body's response to an overactive thyroid gland.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4-6 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried hypericum perforatum aerial parts with 500 milliliters of water for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles for future use.
Mix 10 milliliters of the freshly distilled essential oil with 90 milliliters of jojoba oil and apply topically to affected areas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's citrus scent has been shown to help regulate thyroid function, reducing symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Lemongrass also contains citral, a compound that has been found to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, helping to alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms like fatigue and heat intolerance. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, lemongrass essential oils can provide relief from the physical and emotional discomfort associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass essential oil's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to thyroid inflammation and damage, reducing oxidative stress on the gland.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass essential oil may help reduce swelling and pain associated with hyperthyroidism-related thyroiditis and other inflammatory conditions.

Adrenal balancing properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to have a regulating effect on adrenal glands, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, insomnia, and fatigue by promoting a healthy cortisol-thyroid axis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, roots are used to create an oil that helps reduce inflammation and stress on the thyroid gland.
Rhyzomes: As the main storage organ of the plant, rhyzomes contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and trim them into small pieces, yielding approximately 50 grams of plant material.
Steam distill the lemongrass mixture in a stainless steel still at atmospheric pressure for 2 hours, producing about 15 milliliters of essential oil.
Combine the extracted essential oil with carrier oils like coconut or jojoba oil in a ratio of 1:4 to create a blend suitable for topical application.
Allow the blend to mature for 24 hours before straining it through cheesecloth or coffee filters into dark glass bottles, ready for use.
Store the finished cymbopogon citratus essential oil blend in a cool, dry place, protected from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because of its ability to stimulate the brain and nervous system, promoting a sense of calm and reducing stress. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties may also help protect against oxidative damage caused by thyroid hormone imbalances, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing this condition.

Adaptogenic effects: Rosemary essential oil's adaptogenic properties can help regulate the body's response to stress, which is often exacerbated in individuals with hyperthyroidism, promoting a sense of calm and balance.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate symptoms such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are believed to have a calming effect on the thyroid gland, helping to reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal oil quality and flavor profile.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or oven drying method at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried leaves with a distillation apparatus and pure water ratio of 1:5 for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a paper filter or cloth to remove impurities and sediment adding a few drops of high-proof ethanol as co-solvent.
Store the extracted rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid at room temperature for 6-12 months ensuring stability and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with hyperthyroidism because they have a profound impact on the thyroid gland's function. The anti-inflammatory properties of elder oil reduce swelling and discomfort in the neck, where the thyroid gland is located, alleviating symptoms such as pain and stiffness. Adaptogenic properties: The adaptogenic properties of elderberry essential oil help balance the thyroid gland's function by regulating cortisol levels, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, all of which are beneficial for individuals with hyperthyroidism.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at dawn when they are most potent with a high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Steam distill the plant material using a clevenger apparatus with 10 grams of flowers and leaves in 100 milliliters of water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment within 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight for 2 weeks.
Analyze the essential oil's composition using gas chromatography mass spectrometry (gc-ms) to ensure it meets quality standards for hyperthyroidism treatment within 1 day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperthyroidism with herbal essential oils is to blend Lemon (Citrus limonum) and Thyme (Thymus vulgaris) essential oils. The antithyroid properties of Lemon oil help reduce thyroid hormone production, while Thyme oil's natural anti-inflammatory properties soothe the thyroid gland. Use 3-5 drops of each oil in a carrier oil and massage onto the neck or chest three times a day for optimal results. Always dilute essential oils with a carrier oil and consult with a healthcare professional before use.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperthyroidism that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions such as adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis, and hypothyroidism. Bladderwrack oil, for example, is used to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote its functioning. Additionally, herbs like ashwagandha, licorice root, and lemon balm can help regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions, such as anxiety, insomnia, and weight changes.
